mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) signals strengthening consumer demand, which raises expected corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The effect is monotonic: larger increases produce stronger positive returns.
failure_condition: If in months when RSAFS increases month-over-month, the S&P 500 20-day return is negative more than 45% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals weakening labor market conditions, which reduces consumer income and spending, leading to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in months when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, the S&P 500 20-day return is positive more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increase in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) and decrease in UNRATE (unemployment rate) signals a robust tightening labor market, boosting consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon. The joint movement is required; isolated improvement in one without the other weakens the signal.
regime_hint: expansion with low unemployment and rising payrolls
failure_condition: If in instances where both PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls month-over-month, the S&P 500 5-day forward return is negative more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of high GDP growth (annualized QoQ above 2%), increases in industrial production (INDPRO) indicate that supply is expanding to meet demand, signaling sustainable economic momentum that supports equity prices over the next 60 trading days. In low-growth regimes, the same increase may indicate inventory buildup and be negative.
regime_hint: high GDP growth regime (GDPC1 QoQ > 2% annualized)
failure_condition: If during high-GDP-growth periods, an increase in INDPRO is followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.5%, monetary policy becomes restrictive, increasing the cost of capital and discounting future earnings more heavily, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, the S&P 500 20-day return is positive more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When both housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1200 thousand units and retail sales (RSAFS) exceed 500,000 million dollars simultaneously, the dual signal confirms robust domestic demand. This broad-based strength supports corporate earnings and pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: strong demand from housing and consumption
failure_condition: If in instances when both thresholds are met, the S&P 500 20-day return is negative in more than 45% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing activity, which raises capacity utilization and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the longer 60-day horizon. The signal is persistent across all phases of the business cycle.
failure_condition: If in months when INDPRO rises month-over-month, the S&P 500 60-day return is negative more than 40% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in both real GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates synchronized demand and supply growth, reflecting a self-reinforcing expansion that boosts corporate earnings and equity valuations over the 60-day horizon. The joint signal is more reliable than either series alone.
failure_condition: If in quarters when both GDPC1 increases quarter-over-quarter and INDPRO increases month-over-month, the S&P 500 60-day return is negative in more than 35% of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.
